programme.title: Why Do They Run
programme.desc: Why Do They Run? is a documentary about why sled dogs do what they do-run with such tireless enthusiasm and desire. There are some who are quick to criticize dog mushing without an understanding of the rare capabilities of the sled dog, as well as the extraordinary care given these 4-legged athletes by their musher and the veterinary team of the Iditarod. This thought provoking and compassionate film challenges the viewer to gain a true understanding of why these elite marathon athletes are able to do what they do. This doc provides the viewer with insight into sled dog history, genetics, and the physiology of these supercharged canine athletes.

programme.title: J Docs Presents Liga Terezin
programme.desc: Jewish prisoners played 100s of soccer matches in front of 1000s of spectators in the Terezin ghetto from 1942 to 1944. Exploring how these soccer matches provided a brief escape from the prisoners' reality of hunger, sickness, fear, and death.

Oscar®-winner* Philip Seymour Hoffman (Capote) is Andy, an overextended payroll executive who lures his younger brother, Hank (Ethan Hawke, Training Day), into a larcenous scheme: the pair will rob a suburban mom-and-pop jewelry store that appears to be the quintessential easy target. programme.title: The Blue Realm Ep111: Shark Nation
programme.desc: There's no doubt sharks have an image problem. And they're certainly in trouble globally. The population of some species has declined by more than 90% due to over fishing. But there's still one place in the world where sharks thrive – the Bahamas. Sharks not only prosper there, they are highly protected. It’s illegal to kill them.

programme.title: Let's Take It Outside Ep107: Fish On…Cayman Islands International Fishing Tournament & Wild Cayman Adventures (Part 1)
programme.desc: Hop-aboard as we’re fishing the Cayman Islands International Fishing Tournament hoping to win big. In this episode you’ll also enjoy Cayman Yoga and Wellness Adventures plus plenty of fresh, fine dining.

programme.title: All Stars
programme.desc: The day starts off with some unique courses of fire that get the All Stars’ blood pumping. With the help of Rogue Ops, New Frontier Armory and Griffin Armament, the teams are outfitted with the most premiere artillery in the industry. Then, the All Stars get out of the heat and head indoors to do some CQC training with Surviving Mann alum, Randy Couture. If anyone thought it would be a break.. they were surely mistaken.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Season 3 of Surviving Mann starts with no time wasted... straight to PT. The contestants are thrown straight into physical training, to determine where everyone lies and how to properly group them into the iconic Black and Blue Surviving Mann teams. From Olympic athletes to cast favorites, these eight competitors are ready to prove what they’ve got.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Mike Glover, Green Beret and retired CIA operative, joins Don to kick it up a notch for our contestants. What would Surviving Mann be without some log PT? If it’s good enough for the Seals - it’s good enough for our contestants. If that wasn’t hard enough, Don and Mike have the contestants do some tire pulling, a run, tug of war, land nav and a Tyrolean traverse over alligator infested water. Oh, and did we mention the 40mph wind and rain storm?

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Surviving Mann returns with its most punishing season opener yet. In true Don Mann fashion, the bar is raised with a grueling 10-part physical readiness test—infused with a blast of hypothermia for good measure. Don himself calls it “the most brutal PT [he’s] ever witnessed,” and it’s clear the contestants are in for a world of hurt. To push them even further, MMA legend Ken Shamrock joins the fray, bringing his no-nonsense intensity to whip the group into fighting shape. Those who survive the trial by ice and exertion are rewarded with a chance to test-fire the lightest AR on the market—if they can still feel their trigger fingers.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: For the first time in Surviving Mann history, the contestants undergo intense combat training led by none other than Ken Shamrock—"the most dangerous man in the world." His relentless situation drills push this elite group beyond their limits, sharpening their instincts and forging next-level tactical readiness. As a new mission begins to take shape, the pressure mounts—and the team learns that the strongest among them will earn a game-changing reward. But in a world where every edge matters, not everyone is built to keep up. Fatigue sets in, tensions rise, and the difference between teammate and competitor begins to blur. With Shamrock watching closely, only the most disciplined will rise to meet the challenge—and prove they’re worthy of what lies ahead.

programme.desc: Three-time NASCAR Cup Series Champion and Daytona 500 winner Joey Logano continues to push the limits on the track while finding new ways to use his racing platform to make a positive impact on others. programme.title: S1E1 Tour
programme.desc: Pursuing the Tour de France, one of the most physically demanding sporting events in the world, covers over 3,535 kilometers in just three weeks. Every young cyclist dreams of competing in this prestigious race, but only nine riders from each team make the cut. Young cyclist Adam Yates is giving everything he has to prove he belongs on cycling’s biggest stage. 2016 Tour de France winner Chris Froome reflects on the long and grueling journey to victory, while former professional cyclists and Tour de France veterans Jens Voigt, Christian Vande Velde, and Haimar Zubeldia reveal what it truly takes to compete at the highest level.

programme.title: S1E2 Tour
programme.desc: Pursuing the Tour de France, one of the most physically demanding sporting events in the world, covers over 3,535 kilometers in just three weeks. Every young cyclist dreams of competing in this prestigious race, but only nine riders from each team make the cut. Young cyclist Adam Yates is giving everything he has to prove he belongs on cycling’s biggest stage. 2016 Tour de France winner Chris Froome reflects on the long and grueling journey to victory, while former professional cyclists and Tour de France veterans Jens Voigt, Christian Vande Velde, and Haimar Zubeldia reveal what it truly takes to compete at the highest level.
